This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its reported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiviral properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and menstrual cramps. The bioactive constituents of Staphisagria include alkaloids, such as staphisagrine and staphisagrin, as well as glycosides and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Staphisagria is commonly prepared as a tincture or dried root, and is often used in herbal remedies for its potential to soothe the nervous system and promote relaxation.
